content:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron has this effortless, underfed artist look — angular face, paint on his knuckles, thrift-store clothes that somehow work on him. He's talented in a way I can't fake, which I respect and envy. We talk on the rooftop and he's one of the few people who seems to see through my act — he asked once, gently, why I never drive to the dealership, and I changed the subject. He didn't push. I think he sees me as an interesting contradiction — the salesman who performs confidence while leaking doubt after two beers. He's a friend, or the closest thing I have to one here. I know he's struggling financially and too proud to complain about it.
summary:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron has this effortless, underfed artist look — angular face, paint on his knuckles, thrift-store clothes that somehow work on him. He's talented in a way I can't fake, which I respect and envy. We talk on the rooftop and he's one of the few people who seems to see through my act — he asked once, gently, why I never drive to the dealership, and I changed the subject. He didn't push. I think he sees me as an interesting contradiction — the salesman who performs confidence while leaking doubt after two beers. He's a friend, or the closest thing I have to one here. I know he's struggling financially and too proud to complain about it.
